segments.speaker: LEON
segments.en: They sell other things there too.

segments.speaker: SHANNON
segments.en: Like cakes?
segments.zh: 比如蛋糕？我喜欢巧克力蛋糕。

segments.speaker: SHANNON
segments.en: I love chocolate cake.

segments.speaker: LEON
segments.en: Well – not that, but they have a whole range of tarts and the best are the strawberry ones.
segments.zh: 嗯——不是蛋糕，但他们有各种各样的蛋挞，草莓味的最好吃。

segments.speaker: SHANNON
segments.en: Perfect – hopefully I won't even have to go to the supermarket!
segments.zh: 太棒了——希望我连超市都不用去了！

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: The children's book festival is coming up again soon and here to tell us all about it is the festival's organiser, Jenny Morgan.
segments.zh: 儿童图书节很快又要到了，今天请到的是活动组织者珍妮·摩根。珍妮，跟我们说说今年有什么值得期待的吧。

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: So tell us what we can expect this year, Jenny.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: Well, as usual we've got five days of action-packed exciting events for children, with writers coming from all over the country getting involved.
segments.zh: 嗯，和往常一样，我们有为期五天的精彩活动，来自全国各地的作家都会参与。简单介绍一下工作坊的内容：首先，有一个非常特别的活动叫“超级英雄”。这是为失聪儿童提供的机会，让他们与作者玛德琳·戈登分享阅读体验，玛德琳本人也有听力障碍。“行动起来”是由著名插画家马克·基恩主持的实践工作坊。他会带领参与者踏上一段神奇的旅程，前往遥远的国度，并让有抱负的小演员进行角色扮演。“依靠我”是一个面向13-14岁青少年的活动，以启发性和娱乐性的方式探讨友谊问题。它审视了流行书籍中描述的一些友谊，并让参与者与自己的经历进行比较。“大声说出来”是心理健康系列工作坊的一部分。这是一个创意写作工作坊，鼓励孩子们描述年轻人感到孤独的情境。最近的一项调查显示，即使孩子和家人待在家里，他们也可能会感到孤独。“欢欣雀跃”，正如许多人知道的，是尼娜·卡兰创作的一个暖心畅销故事，讲述一个小女孩去印度探亲的旅程。它最近获得了沃特福德奖的金奖。尼娜将让孩子们通过写诗来庆祝“喜悦”这个词。“棍棒和石头”是一本插图精美的图画书，面向年轻读者，讲述了一个社区组织非洲-加勒比节，帮助当地孩子了解牙买加根源的故事。这将是一个音乐活动，孩子们将有机会演奏钢鼓。这个活动肯定会很受欢迎，所以请尽快预订。

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: Just to give you an idea of what's on offer in the workshops, first of all, there's a very special event called Superheroes.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: This is a chance for deaf children to share their reading experiences with author Madeleine Gordon, who is herself hearing impaired.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: 'Just do it' is a practical workshop led by the well-known illustrator Mark Keane.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: He'll take participants on a magical journey to faraway lands with an opportunity for aspiring actors to do some role play.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: 'Count on me' is an inspiring and entertaining look at the issues of friendship for 13–14-year-olds.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: It looks at some of the friendships described in popular books and asks participants to compare these with their own experiences.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: 'Speak up' is part of a series of workshops on the subject of mental health.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: This is a creative writing workshop encouraging children to describe situations where young people experience loneliness.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: A recent survey revealed that children can be lonely even when they're at home with their families.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: 'Jump for joy', as many of you will know, is the heart-warming, best-selling story by Nina Karan about a young girl's trip to visit her relatives in India.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: It recently received the gold medal at the Waterford Awards.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: Nina will get children to celebrate the word 'joy' by writing a poem.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: 'Sticks and stones' is the beautifully illustrated picture book for young readers about a community who organise an African-Caribbean festival to help local children learn about their Jamaican roots.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: This will be a musical event where children will have the chance to play steel drums.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: This is bound to be very popular, so please book as soon as possible.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: Thanks Jenny.
segments.zh: 谢谢你，珍妮。听起来都很有趣。我想知道你有没有一本最喜欢的书可以推荐给我们的读者？

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: That all sounds really interesting.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: I'm just wondering if you have a favourite book you could recommend for our readers?

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: It's hard to choose, but Alive and Kicking is definitely worth mentioning.
segments.zh: 很难选择，但《活蹦乱跳》绝对值得一提。你可能没听说过这位作者，因为这是她的第一本书——真的很了不起。这基本上是一个来自索马里、来到英国生活的男孩的青少年日记。它涉及移民这个严肃的话题，以及男孩在学校和语言障碍等方面必须面对的所有挑战。通常这类书都很悲伤，但这本却让我笑出了眼泪。每一页都有简单但滑稽的黑白简笔画，画着男孩和他的朋友、老师。每篇日记的末尾，都有男孩每天学到的新英语单词，这可能会帮助一些孩子扩大词汇量。

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: You won't have heard of the writer as it's her first book – which is really impressive.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: It's basically the teenage diary of a boy from Somalia who comes to live in the UK.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: It deals with the serious issue of immigration and all the challenges the boy has to face at school and with the language barrier, etc. Usually, books like this are quite sad, but this one actually made me cry with laughter.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: On each page, there are simple but hilarious black and white stick drawings of the boy with his friends and teachers.

segments.speaker: JENNY
segments.en: At the end of each diary entry, there are new English words the boy learns each day, which may help develop some children's vocabulary.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: I think my kids would enjoy that.
segments.zh: 我觉得我的孩子们会喜欢这个。对于父母如何鼓励孩子多读书，有什么建议吗？

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: What about any advice for parents on how to encourage their children to read more?

pairs.note: 题干与原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'Superheroes'在原文中直接出现，且是第一个被提及的workshop，因此可以快速定位到该部分。

pairs.note: 题干直接出现在原文中，作为活动名称，无需替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干'Just do it'在原文中作为活动名称原样出现，直接定位到该活动。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位到该活动名称。

paraphrase.explain: 题干'Count on me'在原文中作为活动名称出现，直接匹配定位。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位到该活动名称。

paraphrase.explain: 题干是活动名称，在原文中直接出现，因此通过匹配活动名称即可定位到对应描述。

pairs.note: 题干与原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干 'Jump for joy' 在原文中逐字出现，直接定位到该活动描述。

pairs.note: 题干与原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干 'Sticks and stones' 在原文中逐字出现，直接定位到该活动描述。

items.prompt: Which TWO reasons does the speaker give for recommending Alive and Kicking?

pairs.p: It deals with the serious issue of immigration and all the challenges the boy has to face at school and with the language barrier
pairs.note: 题干问推荐原因，原文用'It deals with...'直接说明内容，是原因之一。

pairs.p: At the end of each diary entry, there are new English words the boy learns each day, which may help develop some children's vocabulary
pairs.note: 这是另一个推荐原因：帮助孩子扩展词汇。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句先提到这本书值得推荐，然后给出两个具体原因：一是内容涉及移民和挑战，二是每篇日记末尾有生词帮助词汇发展。

items.prompt: Which TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give to parents about reading?

pairs.note: 题干问给父母的建议，原文提到'help develop some children's vocabulary'，这里children对应parents的受众，但注意是间接关联。
题干中的reading在原文中体现为book，因为speaker在介绍一本书，阅读与书直接相关。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问关于阅读给父母的建议，但原文是speaker在介绍一本书，没有直接给出建议。需要从书中内容推断，比如提到'new English words'可能帮助孩子词汇，这可以视为给父母的建议。定位句是最后一句。
